cover.title: devs felt faster. the clock disagreed.
cover.sub: METR ran the randomized trial most teams skip: 16 experienced devs, 246 real issues, on their own repos.

slides.title: about 19% slower with AI
slides.body: On repositories they knew well. The most rigorous trial we have on AI and developer speed, and it points down.

slides.title: they predicted a 24% speedup
slides.body: Going in. After living through the slowdown, the same devs still believed AI had sped them up by about 20%.

slides.title: felt speed isn't measured speed
slides.body: The gap between the vibe and the clock is the real risk. It's a management problem before it's a tooling one.

slides.title: the slowdown is fixable
slides.body: METR named the causes: stale prompts, weak context, no review gates. That's the operating model, not the model.

cta.title: measure it on your own team
cta.sub: A straight read of your agent setup in your real repo, sources and method included. The prioritized plan is yours to keep.
